summary: Today's sweep leans toward Democracy / press freedom, Democracy / anti-fascism, AI / computing. The strongest items in this run are American Classic review – I defy you not to fall in love with Kevin Kline and Laura Linney’s tender comedy, Survivors of Epstein’s abuse accuse Melania Trump of ‘shifting burden’ on to victims, and Now the FAA says gamers are the answer to its air traffic controller shortage.

mainItems.title: American Classic review – I defy you not to fall in love with Kevin Kline and Laura Linney’s tender comedy
mainItems.summary: There are hints of Ted Lasso and Schitt’s Creek in this delightful series in which an unexpected death triggers all manner of fun and games.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Worth watching for authoritarian drift, selective enforcement, and democratic norms.

mainItems.title: 20-year-old man arrested for allegedly throwing a Molotov cocktail at Sam Altman’s house
mainItems.summary: San Francisco police have arrested a 20-year-old man suspected of throwing a Molotov cocktail at OpenAI CEO Sam Altman's Russian Hill house early Friday morning, The San Francisco Standard reports.

mainItems.summary: Amazon Luna announced a big change on Friday that will prevent players from purchasing third-party games and subscriptions.

mainItems.title: Pilot Program Provides Early Childhood Educators with Rent-Free Business Spaces

mainItems.title: 'How are you using AI?' Your therapist should ask you that question, experts argue
mainItems.summary: A paper in JAMA Psychiatry says mental health providers should ask if patients are using artificial intelligence chatbots, just as they would ask patients about sleep habits and substance use.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to your current interest graph.

mainItems.title: "This is not a game that is about to face some sort of imminent shutdown" - Marathon reportedly had a budget of $200m, and while player numbers are dipping, team morale remains good
mainItems.summary: Marathon reportedly had a development budget of over $200m, and likely over $250m, not including ongoing development costs post-launch on game maintenance and new updates. Read more
mainItems.whyItMatters: Worth your time if you care about design, criticism, and not just hype.

mainItems.title: Tales From the Crypt Will Finally Start Streaming on Shudder in May
mainItems.summary: It's not clear how they did it, but Shudder has finally revived the long-lost piece of horror history The post Tales From the Crypt Will Finally Start Streaming on Shudder in May appeared first on Reactor .

mainItems.title: How governments have tried to hide information about the Iran war online
mainItems.summary: Governments are blocking the internet, banning social media posts and cutting access to commercial satellite images. But experts say that efforts to censor information have had mixed results.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Interesting where engineering, science, and tech infrastructure collide.

youtube.title: Pam Bondi's testimony over the DOJ's handling of the Epstein files postponed
youtube.summary: The House Oversight Committee says the deposition of former U.S. Attorney General Pam Bondi has been postponed. The panel planned to discuss her role in overseeing the release of the Epstein files.

youtube.title: Israeli airstrikes kill 3 more journalists in Lebanon and Gaza
youtube.summary: The Committee to Protect Journalists says Israeli airstrikes have killed three journalists in Lebanon and Gaza. One of the journalists in Lebanon worked for a Hezbollah-affiliated news outlet, while another reported for Al Jazeera in Gaza.

new.title: Scavengers Reign
new.why: Animated speculative sci-fi with strong worldbuilding and a weirder imagination than most prestige SF.

new.title: Sugar
new.why: Noir detective structure with a genre wrinkle and a sensibility that feels adjacent to your crime/strangeness overlap.

new.title: The Lazarus Project
new.why: Time-loop espionage, moral pressure, and speculative plotting — very plausible fit.

new.title: Bodies
new.why: Time-spanning conspiracy/mystery structure that fits your taste for long-arc puzzle storytelling.

older.title: Counterpart
older.why: Probably the single cleanest “James show you may have missed”: espionage + parallel-world sci-fi + adult competence.

older.title: Patriot
older.why: Dry, strange, melancholy spy storytelling with a very distinct voice.

older.title: Utopia
older.why: Paranoid conspiracy energy, formal boldness, and a willingness to get strange and ugly.

older.title: Rubicon
older.why: A low-key conspiracy thriller that leans heavily on intelligence work, paranoia, and institutional atmosphere.

shared.title: Bad Sisters
shared.why: Darkly funny, well-observed, and ensemble-driven in a way that plausibly overlaps with Hacks / Derry Girls / Mare of Easttown energy.

shared.title: Detectorists
shared.why: Gentle, funny, humane, and specific — a softer shared-watch possibility.

shared.title: Astrid et Raphaëlle
shared.why: French procedural with a strong central duo and exactly the kind of foreign-crime appeal Maggie may share with you.

shared.title: Somebody Somewhere
shared.why: A warmer character-driven recommendation if the shared overlap leans more Bear / Good Place than pure detective work.
